summary: Matched a batch of previously stubbed Monster Ball item functions in src/melee/it/items/itmball.c, tightened itmball.h prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, and refined related item variable/special-attribute structs. The PR primarily advanced item decompilation coverage for itmball by using existing item physics, collision, animation-state, accessory, effect, and lifetime helpers. Review caught a function return type mismatch against an item callback typedef and an accidentally committed local objdiff binary.
what_changed: Implemented multiple previously placeholder-marked itmball.c functions including it_80297904, it_802979A8, it_802979D4, it_80297A24, it_80297A54, it_80297ABC, it_80297AF0, it_80297B18, it_80297B40, it_80297B8C, it_80297BB8, it_80297CC4, it_80297DD8, it_80297FC8, it_80297FF8, it_80298060, and it_802980A0.
Updated itmball.h prototypes from broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete signatures using Item_GObj*, void, bool, and s32 where supported by the matched code.
Changed it_802980D4 and it_80298114 return types from s32 to bool in both implementation/header context, aligning with bool-returning item callbacks.
Replaced raw bit extraction from it->xDD4_itemVar.mball.x0 with named bitfields b0 through b7 in itMBall_ItemVars.
Added itMBallAttributes in itCharItems.h with two f32 fields, used through item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes in it_80297CC4 and it_80297DD8.
Added includes needed by the new matches, including forward headers, itmball.h, ef/efasync.h, it_266F.h, and itCharItems.h.

Implemented Monster Ball state behavior using existing item helpers such as Item_80268E5C, it_80272860, it_8026E15C, it_80272980, it_80274658, it_8026E414, it_8026B3A8, it_802725D4, collision bounce helpers, it_80274740, efAsync_Spawn, and Item_8026AE84.

Implemented it_80297CC4 setup/reset logic that clears item velocity, resets flags/state fields, sets the lifetime from special attributes on first activation, spawns effect 0x449, changes animation state, and assigns on_accessory to it_80297DD8.
Implemented it_80297DD8 accessory callback logic that compares xD44_lifeTimer to spec_attrs->x4 and stores the result of it_8027AB64 into the mball b1 bitfield.
smart_moves: Converted the Monster Ball item variable byte into named bitfields, making repeated flag tests and writes match source intent better than manual shifts and masks.
Promoted header prototypes while matching the C implementations, reducing future ambiguity around item callback return types and argument lists.
Reused common item state-transition and collision helper idioms instead of open-coding behavior, including Item_80268E5C for animation/state updates and standard bounce-off-victim/shield paths.
Preserved horizontal-velocity-change checks around it_8026E15C calls and only called it_80272980 when x velocity changed, a pattern likely important for matching and behavior.
Separated special attributes into an itMBallAttributes struct and used it for lifetime/accessory timing rather than leaving raw x4_specialAttributes as untyped data.
Used an on_accessory callback assignment for time-dependent Monster Ball behavior instead of placing the timer check in unrelated state code.
decomp_lessons: When matching item action callbacks, verify return types against the function typedef or state table expectations; a function that returns 0 may still need to be declared bool rather than s32.
Bitfields can be a useful way to express item variable flag bytes when the original code accesses individual bits repeatedly; they also make later matches more readable than shift/mask expressions.
Header cleanup is part of decomp matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete prototypes helps catch type mismatches and can unblock builds.

For item physics states, common matching patterns include applying fall speed with it_80272860, running collision/ground transition helpers, comparing previous x velocity, and then calling it_80272980 only on velocity changes.

For item spawn/activation states, special attributes often need a small dedicated struct even when field names are still offset-based; this provides enough typing to match code and improve future naming.
Keep local tooling binaries such as objdiff out of commits; accidental committed binaries can be the only cause of CI failure even when the code builds.
naming_conventions: New struct name follows item-specific CamelCase-style project convention: itMBallAttributes.
Monster Ball item variable flags were named offset/bit-style as b0 through b7, indicating known bit positions but not yet semantic names.
Special attribute fields remained offset-style x0 and x4, reflecting insufficient evidence for semantic names.
Function names remained address-based it_80297xxx identifiers, consistent with still-unnamed decomp functions.
Function parameters in updated prototypes commonly use arg0 when semantics are not yet known, with Item_GObj* as the concrete type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed function signatures to bool for callbacks that return false/0 to satisfy item callback typedefs and matching expectations.
Used explicit function-pointer casts such as (void (*)(HSD_GObj*)) it_80297CC4 when passing callbacks to helpers with HSD_GObj-based signatures.
Introduced a local f32 zero = 0.0f before clearing velocity components in it_80297CC4, likely to shape generated code for matching.
Stored previous_vel_x before calling it_8026E15C and compared it afterward, preserving control flow around conditional it_80272980 calls.
Used ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and ITEM_UNK_0x1 constants in Item_80268E5C calls where state transitions required different update modes.
Accessed item special attributes through item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes cast to itMBallAttributes* to type raw article data enough for matching.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: it_802979A8 needed to return bool to match the function typedef rather than s32.
review_feedback.resolution: Author updated the types and reported that the build succeeded.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: An objdiff-macos-arm64 file appeared to have been committed by accident and was likely the only CI failure cause.
review_feedback.resolution: Author acknowledged the accidental local tooling commit and removed/fixed it before merge according to the discussion context.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itMBall_ItemVars b0 or b1 to semantic Monster Ball flag names.
Find later PRs that give semantic names to itMBallAttributes x0 and x4.
Search for other item callback review comments about bool versus s32 return types.
Search for later itmball.c matches for remaining placeholders it_80297BF8, it_80297C90, it_80297E28, it_80297E5C, it_80297E60, it_80297E8C, and it_80297F94.
Check whether accidental objdiff binary commits recur in past PR review feedback.
